title: NASA shuts down Voyager 1 instrument to keep probe exploring interstellar space
summary: NASA engineers have shut down another instrument on Voyager 1 to preserve its power, ensuring the spacecraft can continue exploring interstellar space. This decision is part of ongoing efforts to extend the mission's lifespan as the probe's power supply dwindles.

title: 600 rocket landings! SpaceX notches another milestone during Sunday Starlink launch (video)
summary: SpaceX achieved its 600th successful Falcon 9 first-stage landing during a Starlink mission on April 19. The launch, carrying 25 satellites, took off from Vandenberg Space Force Station in California.

title: Scientists found a 'bathtub ring' on Mars. Could it be evidence of an ancient Red Planet ocean?
summary: Scientists discovered a 'bathtub ring' on Mars, which may indicate the presence of an ancient ocean covering a third of the planet. This geological feature could provide evidence of Mars' watery past.

title: Astronauts face grip strength challenge in space and on Earth, scientists find
summary: Scientists discovered that living in space can permanently affect astronauts' brain function, including grip strength challenges both in space and on Earth.

title: Watch the Lyrid meteor shower 2026 online this week with these free livestreams
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower in 2026 can be watched online through free livestreams featuring global night sky cameras. This allows viewers to observe the event from home.

title: A peek at the far side of the moon | Space photo of the day for April 20, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a unique image of the moon's far side during their historic April 2026 flyby. This marked a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: '80s sci-fi cult classic 'The Last Starfighter' finally scores a sequel, but it's a comic book! (exclusive)
summary: The cult classic 80s sci-fi film 'The Last Starfighter' is getting a sequel in the form of a comic book. The announcement has been met with excitement from fans of the original movie.

title: What's the best time to see Lyrid meteors in 2026?
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower is expected to peak in 2026 with potential fireballs and shooting stars. This astronomical event is a highlight for skywatchers and space enthusiasts.

title: 'Dark subhaloes' may explain why galaxies seem to form pre-determined shapes
summary: Dark subhaloes may explain the predetermined shapes of galaxies. These tiny, dark galaxies are observed orbiting larger galaxies like the Milky Way.

title: Only 12 people on Earth saw this 'ring-of-fire' eclipse. Here's how one improvised to capture a once-in-a-lifetime photo from Antarctica
summary: A remote Antarctic research team witnessed a rare annular solar eclipse, the only people on Earth to do so. One scientist improvised to capture the event due to limited resources.

title: NASA on Track for Future Missions with Initial Artemis II Assessments
summary: NASA is proceeding with future missions after the successful splashdown of Artemis II. Engineers are analyzing data from the Orion spacecraft, SLS rocket, and launch pad systems.

title: NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage
summary: NASA rolled out the core stage of the SLS rocket for the Artemis III Moon mission, scheduled for 2027, following the successful Artemis II test flight around the Moon.

title: NASA Invites Media to SpaceX’s 34th Resupply Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for SpaceX's 34th resupply mission to the International Space Station. The launch, part of the Commercial Resupply Services contract, will use a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Latvia signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Latvia's participation during a ceremony in Washington.

title: Wheels Up for X-59
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft achieved a key milestone by flying with its wheels up, marking progress in its test campaign. The experimental aircraft has also completed its highest and fastest flights to date, expanding its operational range.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The document lists various space industry events and gatherings scheduled for 2026, including the 41st Space Symposium, Space Policy Institute events, and awards ceremonies hosted by organizations like The Planetary Society and AAU.

title: NASA’s Hubble Dazzles With Young Stars in Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, showcasing young stars in a shimmering star-forming region 5,000 light-years away. The image, taken in visible light, commemorates the telescope's 36th launch anniversary.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: A quality assessment report by CSDA has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. The report underscores the role of commercial satellite data in advancing scientific applications.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA program will host a vendor-focused webinar on April 29 featuring MDA Space. The event aims to provide insights into the program and MDA Space's role.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries and five launch vehicles during the week of April 20. The busy week highlights diverse activities in the global space industry.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, marking a milestone for the partially reusable launch vehicle. The launch occurred just five months after its second mission.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This development is part of their efforts to prepare for commercial space station operations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion, marking progress in the program's next phase.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the crew's operations.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent NG-23 Cygnus mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. The mission marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector. The specific details of the news are not provided in the given input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on space sector challenges.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received from an optimal location. This milestone marks a significant advancement in the satellite navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting 250+ hours of high-resolution solar corona data. Initial findings reveal solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved guidance or navigation systems. It highlights the significance of these innovations for future space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, demonstrated potential for light-based propulsion in space applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology. This innovation could impact various sectors within the space industry.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking a significant step in international space cooperation. The move aligns with efforts to transition from symbolic support to active contributions in the Artemis program.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX has been awarded a contract to launch the ESA's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars as early as 2028 for $175.7M, but NASA's potential cancellation of its participation in the mission due to budget constraints could impact the project.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market, aiming to vertically integrate and address scale concerns for customers. The company's CFO highlighted the need for large-scale production capabilities in the space industry.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is developing a new spacesuit with the first qualification suit expected to be completed by 2027. The company's chief astronaut, Michael López-Alegría, confirmed that production is ongoing despite external factors.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, breaking Axiom Space's monopoly on NASA's Private Astronaut Mission (PAM) contracts.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has established a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a wave of space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The announcement was made on Tuesday.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget concerns, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the feasibility of proposed financial models.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's stance on a particular issue, while NASA invites them to prove their claims wrong. The dispute was discussed during the Space Symposium.

title: Poland Invests in New Defense GEO Satellite Built by Thales, Airbus, and Radmor
summary: Poland has signed a contract with Thales, Airbus, and Radmor to develop a new Geostationary Orbit (GEO) defense satellite. This investment enhances Poland's space defense capabilities.

title: New Glenn Grounded and AST SpaceMobile Satellite Lost After Launch Anomaly
summary: New Glenn rocket was grounded following a launch anomaly that caused AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite to be placed into the wrong orbit, resulting in the satellite being lost. AST SpaceMobile plans to file an insurance claim for the lost satellite.

title: Ovzon Launches New Mobile Terminal in Small Form Factor
summary: Swedish satellite operator Ovzon introduced a new compact mobile satellite terminal, the Ovzon T8, designed for mobility across air, land, and sea.

title: Inside SatEnlight’s Spiral Staircase of Lasers: Interview with Startup Space Winner Matteo Vismara
summary: SatEnlight, an optical communications technology company based in Milan, Italy, uses a unique multi-layered laser beam arrangement in its terminal. The company was featured as a Startup Space Winner in an interview.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity solutions to airlines. Via Satellite reported key announcements in this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships, recognizing it as a key part of the flying experience.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the third phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing Europe's optical communication capabilities.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats during a discussion in Colorado Springs.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract for space-based air moving target indication (AMTI) development to multiple vendors. Officials, including Meink, indicated low risk in the project.
